desc: Popenguine-Ndayane offers a quieter Senegalese coast experience with bird-rich lagoons, fishing villages, and quick access from Dakar's new airport corridor.
hls: Coastal reserve with seabirds and baobab landscapes
Less crowded alternative to Saly resorts
Good base for Dakar + Petite Côte split stays
Warm sea temperatures most of the year

todo.name: Popenguine Nature Reserve walk
todo.desc: Do a guided trail for birdwatching and coastal cliffs.

todo.name: Ndayane fishing village visit
todo.desc: Watch pirogue launches and fish landing routines.

todo.name: Lagoon canoe session
todo.desc: Take a calm-water paddle in morning conditions.

todo.name: Cap de Naze viewpoint
todo.desc: Hike to rocky headland views over Petite Côte.

todo.name: Seafood lunch at beach camp
todo.desc: Try grilled thiof and yassa in family-run beach spots.

todo.name: Dakar day trip
todo.desc: Use one day for museums and markets in the capital.

wifi.note: Adequate in guesthouses near main road; patchier in reserve-adjacent camps.

prac.fltNote: London has regular nonstop flights to Dakar area airports in high season, while Ireland/other UK cities often connect via Paris, Lisbon, or Casablanca; winter demand is strongest.

airports.name: Léopold Sédar Senghor area (Dakar city) transfers

prac.currency: Senegal local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Beach currents can be strong at exposed points—swim only in locally recommended zones.
Road checkpoints are routine on intercity routes; carry passport copy during excursions.
Weekend traffic leaving Dakar can significantly delay airport-bound transfers.

sweet: Nov-Feb for drier weather and easier coastal hiking; shoulder Mar-Apr still workable before heavier heat.
